titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the David Milch Papers
titlestmt.author: by Rosemary K. J. Davis, Tina Evans, Jim Fisher, and Janet Lopes

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This collection contains 114 published digital objects. And those objects are comprised of 388 digital files.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

Updated by Rosemary K. J. Davis and Tina Evans to add recent acquisition.

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.
Boxes 14-16 (videocassettes): Restricted fragile material. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 17 (born digital): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Boxes 44-116 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of David Milch, 2018, and of David Milch and Rita Stern Milch, 2020.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two groupings: I. March 2018 Acquisition, 1918-2017. II. March 2020 acquisition, 1953-2018.

bioghist.head: David Milch
bioghist.p: David Milch (1945-) is an American writer and television producer. After graduating from Yale University in 1966, Milch went on to write and produce award-winning television shows including "Deadwood," "Hill Street Blues," and "NYPD Blue."

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: David Milch Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of writings, scripts, correspondence, photographs, research files, printed material, videocassettes, born digital materials, audiovisual materials, realia, and other papers documenting David Milch's life and work in television. The papers include materials related to Deadwood, Hill Street Blues, and other television shows written or produced by Milch.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The David Milch Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This accession contains writings, scripts, correspondence, photographs, research files, printed material, videocassettes, born digital materials, realia, and other papers by or relating to David Milch's life and work.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Include scripts, short stories, and writings by David Milch and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includs correspondence and annotated papers related to a book about "Deadwood."

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged into five series: I. Writings, 1960-2018. II. Writings of others, 1985-1990. III. Correspondence, 1960-2017. IV. Personal papers, 1953-2013. V. Audiovisual materials, 1966-2019.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains scripts, writings, correspondence, photographs, personal papers, ephemera, and audiovisual materials related to the life and work of David Milch.
